beats.activity: Wake up, feed the dog, make coffee, check on the sourdough starter.
beats.reasoning: Tuesday morning routine at home in Eastlake. Yesterday's low energy (40/100) suggests a slower, quieter start. The sourdough starter needs attention—part of Amelia's rhythm.

beats.activity: Commute to work by bus, dodging the drizzle.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is still disrupted from yesterday; Amelia takes the bus. SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon doesn't affect her westbound commute into Downtown. Budget 40 min for rainy-day bus ride from Eastlake to Downtown pharmacy.

beats.activity: Work at the pharmacy: fill prescriptions, counsel patients, handle the usual Tuesday rhythm.
beats.reasoning: Full morning and early-afternoon shift. Rainy weather keeps steady foot traffic in the pharmacy. No major events affect her work directly; the SR-520 closure doesn't impact Downtown operations.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and soup at a nearby café, stay warm and dry indoors.
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady until 2pm. Amelia chooses an indoor lunch spot close to work rather than venturing far. Fits her pragmatic response to weather.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift at the pharmacy: restock shelves, answer customer questions, wrap up the day.
beats.reasoning: Second half of shift. Rain clears by 2pm, so evening commute will be easier. Steady work day with no major disruptions.

beats.activity: Commute home by bus, rain clearing as the day ends.
beats.reasoning: Eastbound commute is now clear; SR-520 closure only affected eastbound 9am–noon. Budget 45 min for evening bus back to Eastlake.

beats.activity: Feed the dog, check on the sourdough starter, prep ingredients for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Settle back into the home routine. The starter has been rising all day and needs attention. Amelia uses this quiet time to decompress after a steady work day.

beats.activity: Attend a salsa dance class at a studio nearby.
beats.reasoning: Amelia values time doing things she loves. Low energy yesterday (40/100) but a midweek dance class with friends lifts the mood. Short drive from Eastlake to Capitol Hill (~20 min). She's been baking and working; a social outlet fits her rhythm.

beats.activity: Drive home from dance class, chat with the dog about the evening.
beats.reasoning: Return home from Capitol Hill to Eastlake. Budget 20 min for a calm evening drive.

beats.activity: Wind down: shape the sourdough, tidy the kitchen, make tea, settle in for the evening.
beats.reasoning: Amelia returns to her quiet home rituals. The sourdough dough needs shaping before overnight proofing. Tea, a bit of reading or a show, and reflecting on the day. This wind-down ensures she's already at home before sleep.

diary: Woke to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle—fed Biscuit, checked my starter (looking promising), and caught the bus downtown. The bridge closure had everyone talking, but I lucked out on the route. Work was steady, the usual Tuesday hum of refills and patient questions. Grabbed a warm soup at Piroshky's around one and just sat there for a bit, watching the rain streak the windows. Afternoon flew by restocking shelves and helping folks find what they needed. By evening the rain had mostly cleared, which felt like a small gift. Skipped straight to dance class at Velocity—my hips needed that salsa session more than I realized. Came home buzzing, shaped the sourdough, made some tea. There's something grounding about the rhythm of this day, even when the weather's being moody.
thought_process: The rain kept me grounded today, literally—I stayed indoors most of the shift, which meant I could focus on work without the usual external chaos. The bridge closure was a distant annoyance for others; I felt oddly lucky my bus route avoided it. Work felt routine but necessary; there's comfort in that predictability. I think I needed the salsa class more than I admitted—the gray morning had settled something restless in me, and dancing shook it loose. The sourdough starter's been on my mind all day, a small project that feels manageable. I chose the warmth and routine today over anything ambitious, and that felt exactly right.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
